nodes.context: A street gang based in Southern California, founded in the late 1980s, primarily Cambodian and Southeast Asian members.
A gang formed by Cambodian refugees in Long Beach in the mid-1980s to defend themselves from East Side Longos.
A gang that harassed Cambodian refugees in Long Beach during the 1980s.
An independent gang operating in Chinatown Los Angeles and parts of San Gabriel Valley in the late-1980s, predominantly Chinese and Vietnamese.
A faction formed after Asian Boys Insanity merged with Long Beach Asian Boyz in the early-1990s.
A faction formed in the mid-1990s from the merger of Asian Boys Insanity and Monterey Park Asian Boyz.
A branch of Long Beach Asian Boyz that merged with Van Nuys Asian Boy Style in the mid-1990s.
A gang formed in the late 1980s in Van Nuys, later merged with Van Nuys Asian Boyz.
A gang involved in a fight with Asian Boyz in Lowell, Massachusetts in 2006.
A gang originating in Fairfax County, Virginia, affiliated with West Side Asian Boyz.
A gang in long conflict with Asian Boyz, involved in shootouts in 1993.
A gang that influenced Asian Boyz; listed as an ally in the infobox.
Listed as an ally of Asian Boyz in the infobox.
Listed as a rival of Asian Boyz in the infobox.
Listed as a rival of Asian Boyz in the infobox.
Listed as a rival of Asian Boyz in the infobox.
Listed as a rival of Asian Boyz in the infobox.
Listed as a rival of Asian Boyz in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: In the late 1980s, an internal dispute happened within the Tiny Rascal Gang, and members left to form the Long Beach Asian Boyz.
edges.context: Asian Boyz formed from a splinter of Tiny Rascal Gang.

edges.evidence_quote: The founders of Long Beach Asian Boyz were originally Cambodian refugees who the East Side Longos were harassing during the 1980s.
edges.context: East Side Longos harassed Cambodian refugees who later founded Asian Boyz, indicating conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: One of the Asian gang that integrated with Long Beach Asian Boyz during the early 1990s was the Asian Boys Insanity (ABI).
edges.context: Asian Boys Insanity merged with Long Beach Asian Boyz.

edges.evidence_quote: After Asian Boys Insanity merged with Long Beach Asian Boyz, Asian Boys Insanity formed Monterey Park Asian Boyz sometime the early-1990s.
edges.context: Monterey Park Asian Boyz formed as a result of the merger between Asian Boys Insanity and Long Beach Asian Boyz.

edges.evidence_quote: Then later in the mid-1990s, both Asian Boys Insanity and Monterey Park Asian Boyz (MP ABZ) became what is now known as West Side Asian Boyz (WS ABZ).
edges.context: West Side Asian Boyz formed from the merger of Asian Boys Insanity and Monterey Park Asian Boyz, both linked to Asian Boyz.

edges.evidence_quote: By the mid-1990s, Van Nuys Asian Boy Style merged with Van Nuys Asian Boyz (a branch of Long Beach Asian Boyz) and became one gang.
edges.context: Van Nuys Asian Boyz is a branch of Long Beach Asian Boyz.

edges.evidence_quote: Another Asian gang that integrated with Long Beach Asian Boyz was called Van Nuys Asian Boys or Asian Boy Style (ABS).
edges.context: Asian Boy Style integrated with Long Beach Asian Boyz.

edges.evidence_quote: On August 12, 2006, a fight broke out between Asian Bloods and ABZ gang members at a house in Lowell, Massachusetts.
edges.context: Asian Boyz and Asian Bloods engaged in a fight.

edges.evidence_quote: The group is affiliated with the West Side Asian Boyz gang and high-ranking members have recently been arrested for murder.
edges.context: Reccless Tigers are affiliated with West Side Asian Boyz, a faction of Asian Boyz.

edges.evidence_quote: Many members of the Asian Boyz have been in a long conflict with the Wah Ching gang.
edges.context: Asian Boyz and Wah Ching are in a long conflict.
Crips are listed as allies of Asian Boyz in the infobox.
Menace of Destruction is listed as an ally of Asian Boyz in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Bloods, East Side Longos, Smiley Dragon, Tiny Rascal Gang, Crazy Brother Clan, Vietnamese Boyz, Wah Ching, Fresno Bulldogs
edges.context: Bloods are listed as a rival of Asian Boyz in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Bloods, East Side Longos, Smiley Dragon, Tiny Rascal Gang, Crazy Brother Clan, Vietnamese Boyz, Wah Ching, Fresno Bulldogs
edges.context: Smiley Dragon is listed as a rival of Asian Boyz in the infobox.

edges.context: Crazy Brother Clan is listed as a rival of Asian Boyz in the infobox.

edges.context: Vietnamese Boyz is listed as a rival of Asian Boyz in the infobox.
